name: Madeiran Portuguese

allophone_rules.notes: The diagnostic Madeiran palatalisation of an intervocalic /l/ to [ é] after /i/: quilo -> [Υàki éu], mochila -> [muΥà Éi é…ê], vila -> [Υàvi é…ê]. Conditioned on the preceding lattice slot's phoneme being /i/ and the following grapheme being a vowel (so the /l/ is an onset between /i/ and another vowel), which is exactly the environment reported for the feature and correctly leaves an /l/ after other front vowels untouched (telefΟ©rico -> [t…®l…®Υàf…¦…Ψiku], /l/ after reduced […®]). Source: Segura (2013: ¬ßon the Madeiran dialect).

notes: Madeiran Portuguese (Funchal / Ribeira Brava reference). Modelled features: (1) NASAL DIPHTHONG -> NASAL+N βÄî the salient productive Madeiran pattern in the plural/nasal endings -ΟΒes -> [ΟΒns], -ΟΘes -> […êΧÉns], -ΟΘos -> […êΧÉns] (cΟΘes -> [k…êΧÉns]); encoded as multi-character grapheme overrides. (2) INTERVOCALIC /l/ PALATALISATION after /i/ -> [ é] βÄî the MAD_L_PALATALISATION allophone rule (quilo -> [Υàki éu], mochila -> [muΥà Éi é…ê], vila -> [Υàvi é…ê]). (3) The /v/ ~ /b/ distinction is preserved βÄî Madeiran, like standard European Portuguese and unlike the northern mainland, has NO betacism, so /v/ stays [v] (visitar, levada, novo); the single gold datum vinho -> [Υàbi…≤u] is a lexicalised item, not a productive merger, and is deliberately not modelled. NOT MODELLED (documented limits, not faked): singular -ΟΘo -> [ΟΒns] appears sporadically in the gold (verΟΘo -> [v…®Υà…ΨΟΒns]) but the far more frequent monosyllable sΟΘo keeps [s…êΧÉw], so a blanket -ΟΘo override would regress the base and only the productive plural/-ΟΘes/-ΟΒes pattern is encoded; the reported stressed-/i/ diphthongisation ([i] -> […êj]) is left out because this gold keeps [i] (dia -> [Υàdi…ê], ilha -> [Υài é…ê]) and a blanket rule would regress it βÄî it is a lexically-restricted process best left to a downstream engine; r-diphthongisation (correr -> [kuΥà ¹we…Ψ]) and Filipa -> [Υàf éip…ê] are lexical items; stop aspiration and final devoicing are narrow sub-phonemic detail outside the base scope. Dark coda /l/ […Ϊ] and Lisbon-type coda sibilants [ É]/[ £] are inherited from the pt-PT base allophone rules.

ancestors.notes: Standard European Portuguese; Madeiran diverges in the nasal-diphthong -> nasal+N reduction and the intervocalic /l/ -> [ é] palatalisation after /i/, and otherwise inherits the EP reduction and coda allophony.

sources.publisher: In E. B. P. Raposo et al. (eds.), GramΟΓtica do PortuguΟΣs, vol. I, cap. 3, 85-142, FundaΟßΟΘo Calouste Gulbenkian, Lisboa
sources.notes: Standard reference chapter on European Portuguese dialect variation; describes the Madeiran intervocalic /l/ -> [ é] palatalisation and the nasal-diphthong patterns of the Atlantic islands.

sources.author: Mateus, M.H.M. & d'Andrade, E.
sources.title: The Phonology of Portuguese
sources.notes: Reference phonology for the inherited European Portuguese reduction, dark coda /l/ […Ϊ] and coda sibilants, and the /v/ ~ /b/ contrast.

stress.notes: Acordo OrtogrΟΓfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).
